sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in the left hand, describing it as a dull ache that is constant. He states the pain started approximately one week ago after a minor fall. The patient denies any tenderness to palpation. He reports no numbness or tingling. The patient reports no fever or chills.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 120/80 mmHg. Heart rate is 72 bpm and regular. The left hand is warm and pulses are present. Range of motion is full and without pain. No swelling or erythema is noted.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are most consistent with a musculoskeletal strain of the left hand.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe ibuprofen 600mg every 6 hours as needed for pain. Instruct patient to apply ice pack to the affected area for 20 minutes at a time, several times a day. Advise patient to avoid activities that aggravate the pain. Schedule follow-up in 2 weeks if symptoms do not improve.
